# UC-22.2.26 · NIS2 Network Security Monitoring Coverage by Segment

## Description

Compares each architected network segment’s expected daily log volume (from your expectations register) to the last 24 hours of actually indexed firewall or flow events, surfacing VLANs or DMZ tiers that fell below half of their baseline so monitoring blind spots do not linger unnoticed.

## Value

Policy language is easy; consistent visibility is harder—this control gives programme managers a numeric yes/no per zone for audit conversations, backed by ingestion stats instead of hand-waved network diagrams.

## Implementation

(1) Fill `nis2_segment_coverage_expectations.csv` with `segment_id`, `vlan`, `zone`, `expected_events_per_day`, `nis2_scope`. (2) Align `index` naming in the `case()` stanza with real `index=netfw` silos or add one row per index prefix. (3) Install Stream TA 1809 if IPFIX is your segment witness. (4) Schedule daily; notify networking when `coverage_gap=1`. (5) Quarterly reconcile with ES » Settings » Data inputs inventory.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) not strictly mandatory but recommended for navigation context; Stream TA (Splunkbase 1809) if IPFIX feeds exist.
• Indexes partitioned by zone (`index=netfw_corp`, `index=netfw_dmz`, etc.) or tagged via `host=fw-*`; adjust the `case()` mapping to your standard.
• CSV `nis2_segment_coverage_expectations.csv` uploaded via Settings → Lookups with read restricted to `secgov_admin` **RBAC** role.
• Forwarder fleet on firewalls with `inputs.conf` `splunktcp` or `syslog` stanzas; verify with `btool` on heavy forwarders.
• Baseline maths: compute `expected_events_per_day` after **30 days** of smooth operations.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ensure each segment has at least one choke-point device feeding Splunk. For Palo Alto, set log forwarding to HF with `sourcetype=pan:traffic` in `props.conf`. For Cisco ASA, use `sourcetype=cisco:asa`. If using Stream, configure `streamfwd` modular input with `streamfwdcapture` NIC bindings.

Validate EPS per index using `| tstats count where index=netfw earliest=-24h by index` manually before automating.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
The provided SPL pulls the lookup, joins segment aggregates from `tstats`, derives `expected_eps` for one hour, and flags `<50%`. Tune denominator `/86400*3600` if you prefer **15 minute** windows—multiply accordingly.

Add optional clause `| append [| rest /services/data/indexes` to confirm indexes still exist—guards against typo segments.

Schedule daily 08:00 with `earliest=-25h@h` to smooth midnight skew.

Step 3 — Validate
Pick DMZ segment; pause one collector for **10 minutes**; run search—expect `coverage_gap=1`. Restore feed and confirm next run clears row.

Cross-check totals with firewall vendor System Logs » Log Forwarding counters if available.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "NIS2 coverage map": choropleth by site code (`lookup sites_geo`), table underneath with `segment_id`, `nis2_scope`, last cleared timestamp.

Integrate email to network SOC with deep link `/app/search/search?q=%7C%20loadjob` pattern.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Join drops segments** — use `join type=left` to retain expected rows with null events → treat null as zero via `fillnull events`. • **OTHER bucket crowded** — refine regex in `eval segment_id`. • **tstats permission** — role missing `indexes` → grant `netfw` in authorize.conf. • **False gap during holiday** — add `lookup public_holidays.csv` to zero-out alert. • **Acceleration missing** — if using `datamodel=Network_Traffic` variant, enable acceleration; not required for raw `tstats` but helps cross-checks.


## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_segment_coverage_expectations.csv
| join segment_id [
    | tstats `summariesonly` count where index=netfw earliest=-24h by index
    | eval segment_id=case(
        match(index,"(?i)corp"),"CORP",
        match(index,"(?i)dmz"),"DMZ",
        1=1,"OTHER")
    | stats sum(count) as events by segment_id
]
| eval expected_eps=expected_events_per_day/86400*3600
| eval coverage_gap=if(events < expected_eps*0.5, 1, 0)
| where coverage_gap=1
| table segment_id, events, expected_eps, nis2_scope
```

## Visualization

Table (segments with gaps), Single value (blind segments), Heatmap (EPS by hour), Map (site coverage). Key fields: `coverage_gap`, `events`, `expected_eps`, `nis2_scope`, `segment_id`.
